source.journal_name: Romanian Journal of Information Science and Technology

paper.title: Fuzzy-Based Bow-Tie Framework for Supply Chain Risk Assessment of Restaurant Delivery Service
paper.abstract: Fuzzy bow-tie framework for restaurant supply chain risk assessment. Expert opinion aggregation via fuzzy AHP. Sensitivity analysis for risk mitigation.

verdict.summary: Applied fuzzy methodology for supply chain risk. Triangular fuzzy numbers (TFNs), fuzzy AHP equations are standard and necessary for fuzzy systems methodology. No theater detected.

**Verdict:** Appropriate for fuzzy MCDM.

paper.title: Three-Position Temperature Control of a Nonlinear Process Model with Time Delay
paper.abstract: Nonlinear control system analysis. Descriptive function method for relay systems. Numerical methods with simulation validation. Thermal system application.

verdict.summary: Nonlinear control theory with numerical solution. Differential equations (Eq. 1-3) and harmonic linearization are standard and necessary for describing function analysis.

papers.provenance:

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (Control systems theory)

### Necessity Test

paper.title: Randomness and One-way Functions
paper.abstract: High-quality randomness for inverting one-way functions. Kolmogorov-Chaitin complexity. Borel normality. Experimental validation vs lexicographic search.

verdict.summary: Algorithmic information theory with experimental validation. Complexity definitions and set theory are inherent to Kolmogorov complexity - formalism is required and appropriate for Level 4 theory.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3-4 (Algorithmic information theory)

### Necessity Test

**Verdict:** Formalism required for complexity theory.

paper.title: Long-term Power Generation Prediction in Photovoltaics Using Machine Learning-based Models
paper.abstract: PV power prediction using GRU-SVR-DE hybrid. Seasonal and annual forecasting. PVGIS dataset. Bucharest location study.

verdict.summary: Applied ML for PV forecasting. Standard ML architecture descriptions and evaluation metrics. No unnecessary formalism.

paper.title: Behavioral Modelling of Analog Computing Circuit for a Synapse of any Neuron in Artificial Neural Networks
paper.abstract: Analog multiplying circuit for neural network synapse. Bipolar and MOS implementations. Sub-threshold operation. Low power, real-time processing.

verdict.summary: Analog IC design for neural computing. BJT current equations (Eq. 2-3) are standard and necessary for translinear circuit analysis.

paper.abstract: MLC for NAO robot PID tuning. GA and Bayesian optimization comparison. Digital twin in Gazebo. Transfer learning to physical robot.

verdict.summary: Robotics control with ML and digital twins. Optimization formulations are standard for GA/BO algorithms. Experimental validation on real robot.

paper.title: Carbon Nano-Onions-Based Matrix Nanocomposite as Sensing Film for Resistive Humidity Sensor
paper.abstract: CNOs-PVP nanocomposite for RH sensing. Drop casting on gold IDT. SEM, AFM, FTIR, XRD, Raman characterization. Sensing mechanism analysis.

paper.title: Higher-Order Model-Free Control Tuned by Fictitious Reference Iterative Tuning for 3D Cranes
paper.abstract: MFC-FRIT algorithm combination. Higher-order ultra-local models. Empirical sensitivity study. 3D crane experimental validation.

verdict.summary: Data-driven control theory with experimental validation. Ultra-local model equations (Eq. 1-2) are necessary for MFC methodology. Heavy formalism appropriate for Level 3-4 control theory.

paper.title: Mobile Robot Environment Representation Through Fuzzy Signatures-Integrated Quadtrees
paper.abstract: Fuzzy signatures with quadtrees for robot environment representation. 0.1m resolution in 20mx20m area. 0.5ms inference time. A* pathfinding.

verdict.summary: Fuzzy signatures for robotics environment mapping. Set-theoretic definitions (Eq. 1) are intrinsic to fuzzy signature theory. Appropriate for Level 3 robotics/AI.
